answers.decision: The inventory is complete enough for planning: 29 records, 24 candidates, and 5 held records cover table, bar, line, pie, percentage/index, producer, and elasticity representations. Chart-only and source-annex-dependent cases still need stronger evidence or calibration before mutation.
A61-A84 are acceptable as provisional IDs only. RX.2+ must re-check live A-domain numbering before any mutation.

Start with the low-risk table/index/core-percentage lane: table value selection, base/comparison value identification, table percentage change, percentage-point distinction, table index, and percentage change from index values.

answers.answer: B. Yes, but keep medium/high risk
answers.decision: Chart-only candidates with didactic-prior rationale are acceptable as candidate records, not mutation approvals. Bar, pie, and some composed chart operations remain medium/high risk until stronger evidence or explicit didactic approval exists.

answers.answer: B. Split table and graph into separate sprints or implementation lanes
answers.decision: Producer candidates may remain under the RX.3 umbrella, but implementation should split table/producer-data candidates from graph/profit-rectangle/break-even recognition candidates.

answers.answer: B. Move A82-A84 earlier, with A83 conditional on graph/source-value readiness
answers.decision: Elasticity has strong exam relevance. Move A82 and A84 earlier after the RX.2 core percentage/table lane. Keep A83 conditional on P-Q graph interpretation and source-value readiness.

The five held/duplicate decisions are correct: welfare areas, shift-vs-movement, short side/intervention quantity, marginal table values, and graphical MO=MK overlap existing units enough that minting now would create catalog drift.
Close RX.1 as an inventory/planning gate. RX.2 is planning-unblocked but not mutation-authorized.

pattern_analysis: The answers approve RX.1 as a planning inventory while preserving strict mutation limits. The first RX.2 lane is narrowed to A61, A66, A67, A70, A72, and A74. Chart-only candidates remain higher-risk candidates, producer candidates must split table/data and graph lanes, elasticity A82/A84 moves earlier, and all held duplicate/overlap records remain blocked.
targeted_followups: Re-check live A-domain numbering before any RX.2 mutation.
Plan RX.2 first lane only for A61, A66, A67, A70, A72, and A74.
Keep chart-only didactic-prior candidates medium/high risk until stronger evidence or explicit approval exists.
Split producer table/data and producer graph candidates into separate implementation lanes.
Move A82 and A84 earlier than producer-graph candidates while keeping A83 conditional.
Keep the five held duplicate/overlap records blocked unless a later human review moves one into a mutation queue.
